engineGuide.h2: Volvo XC70 Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Volvo XC70 across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 2.4L Diesel
entries.history: Final evolution of Volvo's 2.4L D5, bringing Euro 5 compliance and refined mapping to the XC70 P3 range.
compatibleVariants: 2.4 D5 SE, 2.4 D5 SE Lux, 2.4 D5 R-Design, 2.4 D5 Momentum, 2.4 D5 Summum
commonFailures: Timing chain tensioner wear - documented 80k-120k miles, causes rattling on cold start
Turbo actuator sticking - electronic wastegate fails, triggers limp mode
EGR valve carbon buildup - restricts flow, increases emissions and rough idle
entries.cta: Get quotes for D5244T15 Volvo XC70 engine replacement

entries.title: 2.4L Diesel
entries.history: Updated D5244T15 with revised ECU mapping for improved emissions and fuel economy on later P3 models.
compatibleVariants: 2.4 D5 SE Lux, 2.4 D5 R-Design, 2.4 D5 Summum
commonFailures: AdBlue system injector clogging - post-2014 units, causes NOx warning lights
Turbocharger bearing wear - high-mileage units above 120k miles
entries.cta: Get quotes for D5244T17 Volvo XC70 engine replacement

entries.title: 2.4L Diesel
entries.history: Early P3 D5 engine, bridging P2/P3 generations with updated injection but pre-AdBlue architecture.
commonFailures: Swirl flap actuator failure - plastic linkage breaks on pre-2011 units
Injector seal degradation - diesel dilution in oil, hard starting when cold
entries.cta: Get quotes for D5244T10 Volvo XC70 engine replacement

entries.title: 2.4L Diesel
entries.history: Definitive P2-generation D5 code, powering the original XC70 with robust but ageing five-cylinder design.
compatibleVariants: 2.4 D5, 2.4 D5 AWD, 2.4 D5 SE, 2.4 D5 Executive
commonFailures: Timing belt tensioner wear - critical service item, failure causes catastrophic damage
Auxiliary belt snap - can damage crank seal and front cover if not inspected
entries.cta: Get quotes for D5244T Volvo XC70 engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Diesel
entries.history: Volvo's Drive-E four-cylinder replacement for the five-cylinder D5, bringing Euro 6 compliance and ULEZ compatibility.
compatibleVariants: 2.0 D4 Drive-E, 2.0 D4 SE, 2.0 D4 Momentum
commonFailures: Timing chain guide wear - early Drive-E units, causes rattling above 90k miles
EGR cooler leakage - coolant enters exhaust, triggers warning lights
entries.cta: Get quotes for D4204T5 Volvo XC70 engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Diesel
entries.history: Transitional four-cylinder D4, introduced to improve fuel economy on early P3 XC70 models.
commonFailures: Injector coding drift - causes rough idle and increased fuel consumption
Turbo wastegate sticking - reduced boost, limp mode activation
entries.cta: Get quotes for D5204T3 Volvo XC70 engine replacement

entries.title: 2.5L Petrol
entries.history: Volvo's high-output five-cylinder turbo petrol, balancing performance and refinement across P2/P3 XC70 generations.
commonFailures: Oil trap (PCV) clogging - causes oil consumption, blue smoke, rough idle
Timing belt tensioner wear - critical service item, failure risks valve damage
entries.cta: Get quotes for B5254T2 Volvo XC70 engine replacement

entries.title: 2.5L Petrol
entries.history: Earlier five-cylinder turbo petrol code, powering P2-generation XC70 with proven but ageing architecture.
commonFailures: Auxiliary belt tensioner failure - can damage front crank seal if belt snaps
Ignition coil wear - misfires under load, especially on high-mileage units
entries.cta: Get quotes for B5254T Volvo XC70 engine replacement

entries.title: 3.0L Petrol
entries.history: Volvo's flagship turbocharged six-cylinder, delivering flagship performance to the XC70 T6 AWD variant.
commonFailures: Turbocharger oil feed restriction - coking causes bearing failure above 100k miles
High-pressure fuel pump wear - reduced power, hard starting when hot
entries.cta: Get quotes for B6304T4 Volvo XC70 engine replacement

entries.title: 3.2L Petrol
entries.history: Naturally aspirated six-cylinder offering smooth, linear power without turbo complexity for P3 XC70 models.
commonFailures: Variable valve timing solenoid wear - causes rough idle and reduced power
Oil sludge buildup - if service intervals extended, risks camshaft wear
entries.cta: Get quotes for B6324S Volvo XC70 engine replacement

commonProblems.h2: Volvo XC70 Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Volvo XC70's D5 diesel and five-cylinder petrol engines dominate UK replacement enquiries. Timing chain tensioner wear on D5244T units and oil trap failure on B5254T engines are the most frequent failure points. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty.

problems.h4: Volvo XC70 Timing Chain Failure - Why D5244T Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.affectedModels: Volvo XC70 2.4 D5 SE, SE Lux, R-Design (2011-2016, D5244T15 & D5244T17)
problems.rootCause: Hydraulic tensioner loses pressure over time, allowing chain slack that causes rattling noise and eventual jump.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace chain, guides, tensioner before catastrophic failure
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if caught early; 40k-60k mile extension

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Chain replacement plus valve timing reset, ECU relearn
repairOptions.longevity: Restores function but doesn't address bottom-end wear

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt unit with upgraded tensioner, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for high-mileage XC70; addresses all wear points

problems.recommendation: On a XC70 worth £3,000-£8,000 with over 100k miles, preventative chain work rarely makes financial sense. A rebuilt D5244T15 with upgraded tensioner and 12-month warranty offers better long-term value and peace of mind.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt D5244T15 engine prices for your Volvo XC70

problems.h4: Volvo XC70 Turbocharger Failure - Common on High-Mileage D5 Units
problems.affectedModels: Volvo XC70 2.4 D5 all variants (2009-2016, D5244T series)
problems.rootCause: Turbo bearing wear from oil coking and heat cycling causes shaft play, leading to boost loss and oil consumption.

repairOptions.longevity: Only viable if turbo internals are confirmed healthy
Extends life 30k-50k miles if block is in good condition

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt unit with new turbo, upgraded oil feed, warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best option if engine has other wear or over 120k miles

problems.recommendation: If your XC70 D5 has over 110k miles and shows turbo whistle or blue smoke, a standalone turbo rebuild is a false economy. The rebuilt engine option includes a new turbo and addresses all wear points for similar net cost.
problems.cta: Get quotes for Volvo XC70 D5 rebuilt engine with new turbo

problems.h4: Volvo XC70 Oil Trap (PCV) Failure - B5254T Petrol Engines
problems.rootCause: Oil trap membrane hardens and cracks, allowing crankcase vapours into intake, causing oil consumption and rough idle.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace oil trap, hoses, breather valves
repairOptions.longevity: Effective if caught early; 30k-40k mile solution

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: PCV replacement plus intake clean, new seals
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses secondary damage from oil vapour ingestion

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt B5254T with updated PCV design, warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value if engine shows compression loss or high oil use

problems.recommendation: PCV failure on B5254T engines is common but often caught early. If your XC70 petrol has under 90k miles and no compression loss, the minor PCV replacement is cost-effective. Above 100k miles or with blue smoke, consider the rebuilt option.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt B5254T2 engine prices for your Volvo XC70

problems.h4: Volvo XC70 EGR Valve Failure - D5 Diesel Emissions Issues
problems.affectedModels: Volvo XC70 2.4 D5 all variants (2009-2016, D5244T series)
problems.rootCause: Carbon buildup restricts EGR valve movement, causing rough idle, limp mode, and failed emissions tests.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Clean or replace EGR valve, reset adaptations
repairOptions.longevity: Quick fix but carbon often returns within 20k miles

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Blank EGR physically, software remap to disable function
repairOptions.longevity: Popular UK solution; check MOT/emissions compliance first

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt unit with EGR delete option, warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best if engine has multiple issues or over 120k miles

problems.recommendation: EGR failure alone rarely justifies engine replacement. However, if your XC70 D5 also shows turbo wear or timing chain noise, bundling repairs into a rebuilt engine often delivers better value and reliability.
problems.cta: Find Volvo XC70 D5 engine replacement with EGR solutions

problems.h4: Volvo XC70 Injector Failure - High-Pressure Diesel System Issues
problems.rootCause: Piezo injector seals degrade, allowing diesel into cylinders or oil, causing hard starting and oil dilution.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace one faulty injector, code to ECU
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if only one injector failed and mileage under 100k

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace all injectors, new seals, ECU coding
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses systemic wear but doesn't fix other engine issues

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt unit with new injectors, warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value if engine has other wear or over 110k miles

problems.recommendation: Injector failure on D5 engines is often a symptom of broader fuel system wear. If your XC70 has over 100k miles or shows multiple injector codes, the rebuilt engine option with new injectors included offers better long-term value.
problems.cta: Get quotes for Volvo XC70 D5 rebuilt engine with new injectors

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Volvo XC70
finalCta.paragraph: The XC70's most common engine failures-timing chain, turbo, PCV, EGR, and injectors-often appear together on high-mileage examples. EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Volvo XC70 specialists who supply rebuilt units with upgraded components addressing these exact failure points. Every engine includes a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty and nationwide delivery options. Compare quotes today and keep your XC70 on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Volvo XC70 Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Volvo XC70 and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.h2: Volvo XC70 Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the Volvo XC70 is listed below, with compatible engine codes, production years and common failure notes to help identify your replacement options.

items.title: Volvo XC70 2.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.0L D4 Drive-E diesel arrived late in the XC70's lifecycle, replacing the five-cylinder D5 to meet Euro 6 emissions and ULEZ compliance. It offers improved fuel economy but requires careful compatibility checking on P3 models.
commonFailurePoints: Timing chain guide wear on early Drive-E units; EGR cooler leakage; injector coding drift
items.cta: Get quotes for Volvo XC70 2.0L diesel engines

items.title: Volvo XC70 2.4L Diesel Engines
items.description: The definitive XC70 diesel displacement, the 2.4L five-cylinder D5 powered most UK examples across P2 and P3 generations. Robust but prone to timing chain tensioner and turbo issues at higher mileage.
commonFailurePoints: Timing chain tensioner wear (P3); swirl flap actuator failure (pre-2011); turbo actuator sticking
items.cta: Get quotes for Volvo XC70 2.4L diesel engines

items.title: Volvo XC70 2.5L Petrol Engines
items.description: Volvo's five-cylinder turbo petrol offered strong mid-range torque for the XC70, balancing performance and refinement. Oil trap (PCV) failure and timing belt wear are the key maintenance considerations.
commonFailurePoints: Oil trap clogging causing oil consumption; timing belt tensioner wear; auxiliary belt snap risk
items.cta: Compare Volvo XC70 2.5L petrol engine prices

items.title: Volvo XC70 3.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The turbocharged 3.0L six-cylinder powered the flagship XC70 T6 AWD, delivering strong performance but requiring careful maintenance of turbo oil feeds and high-pressure fuel systems.
commonFailurePoints: Turbo oil feed coking causing bearing failure; high-pressure fuel pump wear; intercooler pipe leaks
items.cta: Compare Volvo XC70 3.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Volvo XC70 3.2L Petrol Engines
items.description: The naturally aspirated 3.2L six-cylinder offered smooth, linear power without turbo complexity for early P3 XC70 models, though oil sludge can develop if service intervals are extended.
commonFailurePoints: Variable valve timing solenoid wear; oil sludge buildup from extended service intervals
items.cta: Compare Volvo XC70 3.2L petrol engine prices

fuelTypes.h2: Volvo XC70 Engines by Fuel Type
fuelTypes.intro: Choose your Volvo XC70 engine by fuel type to compare realistic replacement prices, known issues, and compatible UK supply options - from early 2.4 D5 to the latest 2.0 D4 Drive-E.

items.title: Volvo XC70 Diesel Engines
items.description: Diesel dominates XC70 replacement enquiries, with the 2.4L D5 five-cylinder accounting for most UK searches due to timing chain and turbo failure patterns.
items.descriptor: Diesel dominates XC70 replacement enquiries, with the 2.4L D5 five-cylinder accounting for most UK searches due to timing chain and turbo failure patterns.
items.familiesLabel: Common Volvo XC70 Diesel Engine Families
families: D5244T15 - most searched XC70 diesel code (P3 2011-2016), timing chain tensioner risk
D5244T10 - early P3 D5 (2009-2011), swirl flap actuator failures common
D4204T5 - Drive-E four-cylinder (2013-2016), ULEZ compliant but chain guide wear reported
knownFor: Strong motorway fuel economy and towing capability
Timing chain tensioner failures between 80k-120k miles on P3 D5 units
High UK replacement demand on 2.4 D5 SE and SE Lux variants
items.cta: Get quotes for Volvo XC70 Diesel replacement engines

items.title: Volvo XC70 Petrol Engines
items.description: Petrol XC70 engines offer smoother refinement but lower fuel economy, with oil trap and timing component wear driving most replacement searches.
items.descriptor: Petrol XC70 engines offer smoother refinement but lower fuel economy, with oil trap and timing component wear driving most replacement searches.
items.familiesLabel: Common Volvo XC70 Petrol Engine Families
families: B5254T2 - five-cylinder turbo (2007-2010), oil trap clogging common above 80k miles
B6304T4 - 3.0L turbo six (2010-2016), turbo oil feed coking on high-mileage units
B6324S - 3.2L NA six (2007-2010), simpler maintenance but oil sludge risk
knownFor: Smoother, quieter operation than diesel equivalents
Oil trap (PCV) failure causing consumption on B5254T engines
Lower replacement volume than diesel but higher per-unit cost

items.title: Volvo XC70 Hybrid / Electrified Engines
items.description: No factory hybrid or electrified XC70 variants were sold in the UK; all models use conventional diesel or petrol powertrains.
items.descriptor: No factory hybrid or electrified XC70 variants were sold in the UK; all models use conventional diesel or petrol powertrains.
items.familiesLabel: Common Volvo XC70 Hybrid / Electrified Engine Families
importantNotes: XC70 was discontinued before Volvo introduced mild-hybrid technology
Aftermarket hybrid conversions are not recommended for engine replacement purposes
ULEZ compliance depends on original engine emissions rating, not electrification

engineYears.h2: Volvo XC70 Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the XC70's UK production timeline, organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt prices from specialists.

years.description: The original XC70 launched on Volvo's P2 platform with robust five-cylinder diesel and petrol engines.
keyChanges: XC70 launched as raised, AWD variant of V70 estate
D5244T diesel introduced with timing belt drive
B5254T petrol turbo offered with AWD option
Facelift in 2004 brought revised styling and interior
knownFor: Timing belt critical service item - failure causes catastrophic damage
Strong towing capability and all-weather confidence
years.cta: Get quotes for 2000-2007 Volvo XC70 replacement engines
years.ticker: Volvo XC70 2.4 D5 engine replacement • XC70 timing belt replacement cost • XC70 2.5T oil trap failure

years.description: All-new P3 platform brought modern styling, improved safety and updated engine management to the XC70.
keyChanges: New P3 platform with enhanced crash structure and electronics
D5244T10 diesel introduced with updated injection and mapping
B5254T2 petrol refined for emissions and drivability
B6324S 3.2L naturally aspirated petrol added to range
knownFor: Improved refinement and interior quality over P2
Swirl flap actuator failures on early P3 D5 units
Oil trap issues persist on B5254T2 petrol engines
years.cta: Get quotes for 2008-2010 Volvo XC70 replacement engines
years.ticker: Volvo XC70 P3 D5 engine replacement • XC70 swirl flap actuator fix • XC70 3.2 oil sludge prevention
enquiries: Volvo XC70 P3 D5 engine replacement

years.description: The definitive XC70 engine code D5244T15 arrived, bringing Euro 5 compliance but also the timing chain tensioner issues that dominate UK replacement searches.
keyChanges: D5244T15 introduced with revised timing chain architecture
Euro 5 emissions compliance across diesel range
Revised interior trim and infotainment options
AWD Haldex system updated for improved response
mainEngines: D5244T15 - 2.4L diesel (205-215hp) (2.4 D5 SE, SE Lux)
knownFor: D5244T15 becomes most searched XC70 engine code for replacement
Timing chain tensioner wear emerges as key failure point
T6 AWD offers flagship performance but complex maintenance
years.cta: Get quotes for 2011-2013 Volvo XC70 replacement engines
years.ticker: Volvo XC70 D5244T15 timing chain • XC70 D4 engine replacement • XC70 T6 turbo failure cost

years.description: Final XC70 models introduced Euro 6 compliance, AdBlue systems and the four-cylinder D4204T5 Drive-E diesel to meet tightening emissions rules.
keyChanges: D4204T5 Drive-E four-cylinder diesel introduced for ULEZ compliance
AdBlue SCR system added to late D5244T17 units
Final production year 2016; XC70 replaced by V90 Cross Country
knownFor: Drive-E engines offer ULEZ compliance but new failure patterns
AdBlue injector clogging on late D5244T17 units
High demand for rebuilt D5244T15 as most common replacement code
years.cta: Get quotes for 2014-2016 Volvo XC70 replacement engines
years.ticker: Volvo XC70 D4 Drive-E replacement • XC70 AdBlue system fix • XC70 ULEZ compliant engine

faq.h2: Volvo XC70 Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Volvo XC70 engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: Typical rebuilt Volvo XC70 engine prices range from £1,795 to £3,200 supply-only, depending on engine code and condition. Supply & fit adds £650-£950 labour.
keyPoints: D5244T15 rebuilt units typically £1,950-£2,850
Used engines start around £1,795 but vary by mileage
Supply & fit labour typically £650-£950 nationwide
Prices include VAT and 12-month warranty standard
items.cta: Compare Volvo XC70 engine replacement prices now

items.question: What is the most common Volvo XC70 engine problem?
items.answer: Timing chain tensioner failure on D5244T15/D5244T17 engines is the most frequent catastrophic failure, typically occurring between 80k-120k miles.
keyPoints: Rattling noise on cold start is the key warning sign
Ignoring it risks valve timing jump and piston damage
Rebuilt engines include upgraded tensioners as standard
Prevention is rarely cost-effective above 100k miles
items.cta: Get quotes for rebuilt D5244T15 Volvo XC70 engine with upgraded tensioner

items.question: Are Volvo XC70 engines reliable after replacement?
items.answer: Yes - professionally rebuilt XC70 engines with upgraded components addressing known failure points typically deliver 80k-120k miles of reliable service with proper maintenance.
keyPoints: Rebuilt units include new bearings, seals and gaskets throughout
Upgraded tensioners and turbo components where applicable
12-month unlimited mileage warranty standard across network
Follow service intervals strictly for maximum longevity
items.cta: Find Volvo XC70 rebuilt engines with 12-month warranty

items.question: Can I fit a different engine code in my Volvo XC70?
items.answer: Only engine codes verified as compatible with your XC70's chassis, transmission and ECU should be fitted - cross-reference using your VIN before ordering.
keyPoints: P2 and P3 engines are not interchangeable without major modification
AWD models require Haldex-compatible engine variants
ECU coding may be needed for certain code swaps
Use our VIN checker to confirm compatibility instantly
items.cta: Check Volvo XC70 engine code compatibility with your VIN

items.question: How long does Volvo XC70 engine replacement take?
items.answer: Supply-only engines typically dispatch within 2-5 working days; supply & fit bookings take 3-7 days depending on garage availability and engine stock.
keyPoints: Common codes like D5244T15 often available for next-day dispatch
Rare codes may require 7-10 day sourcing time
Supply & fit includes removal, fitting, fluids and initial run-in
Emergency delivery options available for urgent replacements
items.cta: Get urgent Volvo XC70 engine replacement quote today

items.question: Do Volvo XC70 replacement engines come with warranty?
items.answer: Yes - every engine supplied through EngineMarket includes a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty as standard across our UK specialist network.
keyPoints: Warranty covers parts and labour for manufacturing defects
Keep service records to maintain warranty validity
Some suppliers offer extended 24-month options at extra cost
Always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier before purchase
items.cta: Compare Volvo XC70 engine warranty terms from UK specialists

items.question: Should I repair or replace my Volvo XC70 engine?
items.answer: If repair costs exceed 60% of a rebuilt engine price or your XC70 has over 100k miles, replacement typically offers better long-term value and reliability.
keyPoints: Get itemised repair quote before deciding
Rebuilt engines address all wear points, not just the failed component
Consider your XC70's residual value and intended ownership period
Factor in warranty coverage when comparing options
items.cta: Get repair vs replace cost comparison for your Volvo XC70

items.question: Where can I get Volvo XC70 engine supply and fit near me?
items.answer: Enter your postcode in our checker to find certified Volvo XC70 engine fitters within 25 miles, with verified reviews and transparent pricing.
keyPoints: Network covers all UK mainland postcodes
All fitters vetted for XC70-specific experience
Supply & fit packages include warranty on parts and labour
Mobile fitting options available in select regions
items.cta: Find Volvo XC70 engine supply & fit near your postcode

items.question: What's the difference between rebuilt and used Volvo XC70 engines?
items.answer: Rebuilt XC70 engines are professionally restored with new wear components and warranty; used engines are second-hand units with mileage-dependent condition and shorter warranty.
keyPoints: Rebuilt: new bearings, seals, gaskets; upgraded failure-point components
Used: verify compression test, service history and mileage before purchase
Rebuilt typically £200-£400 more but includes 12-month warranty
Used suits budget fixes; rebuilt suits long-term ownership plans
items.cta: Compare rebuilt vs used Volvo XC70 engine prices

items.question: Is my Volvo XC70 engine ULEZ compliant after replacement?
items.answer: ULEZ compliance depends on your XC70's original emissions rating, not the replacement engine - confirm Euro 5/6 status via your V5C or VIN before purchase.
keyPoints: D4204T5 Drive-E engines (2013+) are typically Euro 6 compliant
Most D5244T15 units are Euro 5; check your specific vehicle
Replacement engine must match or exceed original emissions standard
Non-compliant engines incur £12.50/day ULEZ charge in London
items.cta: Check if your Volvo XC70 engine is ULEZ compliant
